text.title: microservices-design Failure Modes
text.summary: Migrated antipatterns for microservices-design as failure modes. Add detector + repair clauses where missing.

failure-mode.description: Services call each other synchronously for every operation, creating a chain where all services must be up for any request to succeed.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Availability multiplies: if each service is 99% up, a chain of 5 synchronous calls is only 95% available. This defeats the resilience benefit of microservices.

failure-mode.description: Multiple services query the same database tables directly, sharing schema ownership implicitly.
failure-mode.detector: TBD — observable signal that this antipattern is happening
failure-mode.repair: Schema changes in one service break all other services sharing the table. This creates a hidden coupling that makes independent deployment impossible.
